LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PAUL CROMWELL PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (CODE CIV. PROC. 1085) OFF CALENDAR. THE PETITION IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. PETITIONER FAILED TO (1) FILE A NOTICE OF PETITION, (2) PROVIDE AT LEAST 16-COURT DAY NOTICE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 1005(B), AND (3) PROVIDE A POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1113. THIS HEARING IS ALSO PREMATURE BECAUSE PETITIONER RECENTLY SERVED THE PETITION AND RESPONDENT'S RESPONSE (DEMURRER, ANSWER, ETC.) IS NOT YET DUE. (CCP 1089, 1089.5.) VISITING JUDGE: CINDEE MAYFIELD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/CM)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Respondents San Francisco Human Services Agency, City and County of San Francisco, Office of the Mayor-San Francisco, and the City Attorney's Office's unopposed Motion To Dismiss is GRANTED.

Respondents move under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(f)(2). Under this section, "[t]he court may dismiss the complaint as to that defendant when: [e]xcept where Section 597 applies, after a demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend, the plaintiff fails to amend it within the time allowed by the court and either party moves for dismissal."

The court sustained Respondents' demurrer with leave to amend. Petitioner Paul James Galligan Cromwell did not file an amended complaint within the time frame set out in the order. On Respondents' motion, the court orders this action dismissed.

Prior to the hearing, Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Code Civ. Proc. 1085)
Off Calendar Dec-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PAUL CROMWELL Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Code Civ. Proc. 1085)

Off calendar. The petition is procedurally improper. Petitioner failed to (1) file a notice of petition, (2) provide at least 16-court day notice in compliance with CCP 1005(b), and (3) provide a points and authorities in compliance with CRC 3.1113. This hearing is also premature because petitioner recently served the petition and respondent's response (demurrer, answer, etc.) is not yet due. (CCP 1089, 1089.5.)

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CM)
